ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 3-in-1 white coffee subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers instant coffee powder; no dedicated 3-in-1 premix subcode exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for 3-in-1 instant white coffee premix identified in published SNOMED CT release.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)
soy (non-dairy creamer may contain soy lecithin)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 30 g of powder) dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water, yielding a ready-to-drink cup of white coffee.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Instant White Coffee — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 flavoured white coffee (cr_id D09010010). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 flavoured white coffee in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 53 based on the closest analogous entry: instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer, GI driven primarily by sucrose and maltodextrin content. GI 53 falls in the low category (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per 30 g sachet serving calculated as: GL = GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 53 × (13.56 g × 30/100) / 100 = 53 × 4.068 / 100 = 2.16 ≈ 2.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (8.08 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite low sodium (36 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit 3-in-1 white coffee due to its sugar-sweetened beverage classification; switching to an unsweetened white coffee or plain black coffee is recommended to eliminate the independent BP-raising effect of liquid sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.08 g sugar/100 g and 13.56 g total carbohydrate/100 g with only 0.48 g dietary fibre, producing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as the ADA classifies all sugar-sweetened beverages as unsuitable for glycaemic management; a no-added-sugar white coffee variant or unsweetened black coffee should be substituted.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Palm-based non-dairy creamer contributes saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g), and the high sugar content (8.08 g/100 g) raises triglyceride and LDL risk when consumed regularly.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid 3-in-1 white coffee due to its saturated fat from palm-based creamer and high sugar load, both of which are independently associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased CVD risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory combination of high sugar (8.08 g/100 g) and palm-based saturated fat in creamer promotes inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should switch to unsweetened white coffee or plain black coffee to avoid the pro-inflammatory effects of added sugar and palm oil-derived saturated fat present in this premix.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Flavoured White Coffee is an ultra-processed instant beverage popular in Singapore and Malaysia, sold as a single-serve sachet containing a pre-blended mixture of instant white coffee, non-dairy creamer (typically palm oil-based), refined sugar, and artificial flavourings. It is prepared by dissolving the sachet contents in 150–180 ml of hot water, requiring no additional ingredients. The resulting drink is sweet, creamy, and mildly coffee-flavoured with a smooth, light-bodied texture distinct from traditional espresso-style coffee. White coffee refers to coffee beans roasted with palm oil margarine at lower temperatures than conventional dark roasts, producing a lighter colour and milder, less bitter flavour profile. Per 100 g of powder, the product provides 78 kcal, 0.51 g protein, 2.41 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), 13.56 g carbohydrate, 8.08 g sugar, 0.48 g dietary fibre, and 36 mg sodium. Clinically, this is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its 8.08 g/100 g sugar content, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The palm oil-derived saturated fat in the creamer contributes to LDL cholesterol elevation risk. It is commonly consumed at breakfast in Singapore and Malaysia and is widely available in supermarkets, convenience stores, and coffee shops throughout Southeast Asia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 white coffee?
Is instant white coffee suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of premix white coffee?
What is the difference between white coffee and black coffee?
How much fat is in instant white coffee premix?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and secondary nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010010) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, reducing overall GI confidence but not overall record confidence. All mandatory multilingual names and aliases are present.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific 3-in-1 flavoured white coffee premix are not available in the HPB Foodo database entry (D09010010) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 2.10 g/100 g based on typical palm-based non-dairy creamer composition consistent with HPB database analogous entries. calcium_mg reported as 0.0 and iron_mg reported as 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D09010010). GI value of 53 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI 53 is classified as low (GI ≤ 55 per University of Sydney GI Database classification). Glycemic load per 30 g sachet serving = 53 × (13.56 × 0.30) / 100 = 2.2. dietary_tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from input preserved in classification context; 'low_calorie' is borderline at 78 kcal/100 g powder but reflects diluted beverage context.
